flag: <opt-in flag if the program ships behind one, else omit>
wave0_rule: Claim source of truth = `git status` re-run at dispatch time; every dirty file is claimed by in-flight work; units listing a claimed file hard-gate on its land and begin with an explicit rebase-first step.
integration_protocol: Per wave: merge unit branches in the stated order -> python scripts/regen_all.py --write ONCE -> ruff check --fix / ruff format / mypy src/hpc_agent -> targeted batteries incl. named -m slow legs -> push -> GitHub CI matrix green -> append the wave's enforcement-map rows to docs/internals/engineering-principles.md -> record deltas in telemetry.md. Units never commit regenerated artifacts.

_field_schema.unit_id: string. Stable id used in the memo, wave plan, and enforcement-row assignment (e.g. '1.3', 'D-CORE').
_field_schema.wave: string or int. Opaque grouping key; units sharing a wave dispatch in parallel and MUST be file-disjoint. The checker errors on same-wave file collisions and warns (with wave order) on cross-wave overlap. Be consistent within a package.
_field_schema.title: string. One-line human title (matches the wave-plan line in the memo).
_field_schema.files: string[]. The unit's EXCLUSIVE claim — every file/dir it may create or edit. No two same-wave units may share an entry. Mark brand-new paths with a ' (new)' suffix. Directories end in '/'. Prose annotations go in trailing parens; globs (test_io*.py) are allowed. THIS is what disjointness is checked on — keep it matching what the unit actually touches (drift = silent bug).
_field_schema.forbidden_files: string[]. Files this unit must NOT touch even though they are nearby/tempting — usually another unit's exclusive claim or a seam owned elsewhere. Overlap with another unit's `files` is INTENTIONAL here (that is the point) and never an error.
_field_schema.design_constraints: string[]. The binding calls from the memo's settled-design table that this unit builds to. One entry per constraint; cite the delta/finding (e.g. 'delta3', 'A1', 'row L521').
_field_schema.acceptance_tests: string[]. Observable pass conditions — the fire paths and pin FLIPs that prove the unit did its job. Prefer 'X counted via fake', 'seeded Y goes red', 'pins FLIP: <test>'.
_field_schema.test_batteries_to_run: string[]. The exact pre-push targeted test dirs/files + named lints/checks (matches the memo's §4 battery row). Never the full suite locally.
_field_schema.regen_required: bool. True if the unit changes a generator input (CliShape, wire model, primitive, skill) so `regen_all.py --write` must run at wave integration.
_field_schema.merge_risk: string. LOW | MEDIUM | HIGH — how hazardous this unit is to integrate (shared seam, last-in-queue on a hot file, elicitation machinery). Drives merge ordering and reviewer attention.

_optional.claims: string[]. (latency-style) The sweep-docket claim ids this unit closes.
_optional.enforcement_map_rows: string[]. Verbatim enforcement-map row text(s) this unit owes at integration, fire path named.

_EXAMPLE_delete_me.title: Example: PEP-562 lazy __init__ + eager-import smoke contract test (merges LAST; full-CI gate)
design_constraints: Smoke is a default-tier contract test, not a workflow step (covers 3.10/3.11/Windows legs).
The eager smoke imports EVERY lazily-deferred symbol (guard-can-fire applied to the smoke itself).
__getattr__ serves underscore attrs and raises honest AttributeError/ImportError, never swallows.
Merge gate = full CI matrix; merges last in its wave so a red bisects trivially.
acceptance_tests: Eager smoke red when a deferred name is removed from it (seeded).
